description: This figure presents combined atom probe tomography (APT) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) characterization of κ-carbides in Fe-30Mn-1.2C-8Al low-density austenitic steel aged at 600 °C for 24 h. The first subfigure is APT 3D reconstruction: purple iso-surfaces (9 at.% C) represent L'12 structured κ-carbides, with cyan, magenta, gold dots marking C, Mn, Al atoms respectively, and [001], [100] crystallographic orientations labelled. The second subfigure shows element concentration profiles along the 100 nm-length APT analysis cylinder, revealing alternating austenite (γ) and κ-carbide regions: Fe concentration is higher in γ matrix, while Mn, Al, C are obviously enriched in κ-carbides. The third subfigure is bright-field TEM image with 20 nm scale bar, displaying uniformly distributed nanosized cuboidal/rod-shaped κ-carbides (~20 nm size) in austenite matrix, matching APT results. This figure validates the formation of thermally stable nano κ-carbide precipitates during aging, which serve as key strengthening phases for the alloy.
